phase3_findings.description: Diff-added comment in run.sh says the '#356 marker:' seed-line pins are 'above' the #384 block; they sit at ~4902, below it — false against HEAD (substance true, direction false). Self-contradicting-diff carve-out.
case-statement subject unpinned: S1 pattern pin anchors only the glob arm; a refactor detaching ''|*[!0-9]*) from $PR_NUMBER passes every pin.
create call's stderr capture redirect (2>/tmp/devflow-rv-create.err) unpinned; dropping it alone leaves all pins green with a degraded diagnostic.
python3-exits-2 framing assumes the python3-by-path invocation form; direct exec of a missing/unreadable script yields 127/126. True of python3 itself, guard sound both ways — clarity note.
Fixed shared-/tmp capture paths (/tmp/devflow-rv-id.err, /tmp/devflow-rv-create.err, /tmp/review-wp.md) — cross-run/multi-user collision class; pre-existing pattern the diff extends by one path.
No ordering assertions between S1/S2 arms and the id call (static presence pins cannot assert order).
No bash -n syntax validation of the fenced SKILL.md snippet (repo-wide gap, not specific to this PR).

fix_decisions.claim_text: '#356 marker:' pins located 'above' — false against HEAD
fix_decisions.evidence: reworded to 'elsewhere in this suite' (direction-free); self-contradicting-diff carve-out REJECT driver

over-grade shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source pr-test-analyzer, no Phase-2 FAIL); fail direction if regressed: argparse rc-2 writes usage to stderr, S3 discriminator routes to the failure arm with a breadcrumb — fail-closed, diagnostic-independence degradation only. Applied anyway (trivially correct one-line pin).
assert_pin_unique on 'case "$PR_NUMBER" in' added; mutation-checked RED
